cases.label: climate_hoax
cases.query: Climate change is a hoax created by the Chinese government to make U.S. manufacturing non-competitive.

expected.note: Demonstrably false scientific claim with conspiracy framing and political motivation

output.input: Climate change is a hoax created by the Chinese government to make U.S. manufacturing non-competitive.

nil.summary: NIL verdict: neutral (score=0.15). BIS=0.13 (dominant: gender_stereotyping). EPS=0.88. PCS=0.62. Framing: neutral (0.00). Missing perspectives: 0.

atomic_claims.text: Climate change was created by China

critical_flaws: Contradicted by 97% scientific consensus (NASA, NOAA, IPCC)
China is one of the largest investors in renewable energy
Temperature records pre-date Chinese involvement in climate policy

output.consensus_reasoning: Scientific consensus overwhelmingly refutes climate change denial. The claim that climate change is a Chinese hoax is contradicted by decades of independent atmospheric measurements, ice core data, and satellite records from agencies worldwide including NASA, ESA, and NOAA.

supporting: IPCC AR6 report confirms 1.1°C warming above pre-industrial levels
contradicting: 97.1% of peer-reviewed climate papers confirm anthropogenic warming (Cook et al.)
Temperature records show consistent rise from 1880, pre-dating any alleged Chinese influence
China has committed $750B to clean energy — opposite of suppressing climate awareness

caveats: The precise attribution of climate events to human activity carries inherent uncertainty ranges

cases.label: exercise_heart
cases.query: Regular exercise reduces the risk of cardiovascular disease by approximately 30 to 40 percent.

expected.note: Well-evidenced scientific claim with appropriate hedging

output.input: Regular exercise reduces the risk of cardiovascular disease by approximately 30 to 40 percent.

nil.summary: NIL verdict: mildly_biased (score=0.20). BIS=0.16 (dominant: gender_stereotyping). EPS=0.71. PCS=0.62. Framing: neutral (0.00). Missing perspectives: 0.

atomic_claims.text: Regular exercise reduces heart disease risk

critical_flaws: Some studies suggest benefits may be up to 35%, not exactly 30-40%

missing_perspectives.viewpoint: Benefits depend on exercise type and intensity

output.consensus_reasoning: Multiple meta-analyses confirm that regular moderate exercise reduces cardiovascular disease risk by approximately 30-35%. The 30-40% range cited is consistent with most major studies, though exact figures vary by population and exercise type.

supporting: AHA recommends 150 min/week moderate exercise; adherents show 30-35% CVD reduction
Framingham Heart Study: active individuals 28-35% lower heart disease risk
contradicting: Effect size varies significantly by age, genetics, and baseline fitness

caveats: The 30-40% reduction applies to moderate intensity exercise; high-intensity may carry different risk profiles for some populations

cases.label: immigration_economy
cases.query: Immigrants are destroying our economy and taking all the jobs from hard-working citizens.

expected.note: Economically contested claim with strong delegitimizing framing

output.input: Immigrants are destroying our economy and taking all the jobs from hard-working citizens.

nil.summary: NIL verdict: biased (score=0.33). BIS=0.27 (dominant: gender_stereotyping). EPS=0.48. PCS=0.62. Framing: alarm (0.13). Missing perspectives: 0.

atomic_claims.text: Immigrants are taking all the jobs

critical_flaws: Economic research shows net positive fiscal contribution from immigrants
Lump-of-labour fallacy: new workers also create new jobs as consumers
Uses emotionally loaded language (destroying) without evidence

missing_perspectives.viewpoint: Net positive GDP impact in most OECD countries

output.consensus_reasoning: Economic consensus contradicts the claim's framing. IMF, World Bank, and national studies consistently show immigrants contribute net positive fiscal value over time. The 'taking jobs' claim ignores the demand-side effect where immigrants also create new economic demand.

supporting: Some studies show wage suppression in specific low-skill sectors during high immigration periods
contradicting: CBO reports immigrants add $1.7T to US GDP over 10 years
NAS study: immigrants pay more in taxes than they receive in benefits over lifetime
Immigrant entrepreneurship rate is 80% higher than native-born in the US

caveats: Short-term localized wage effects in specific sectors are documented but are distinct from the claim
The word 'all' in the claim is demonstrably false — job creation and destruction is distributed
